data.title: HHS officials to pin 25 child deaths to COVID shots at upcoming meeting - report
data.description:
Trump administration health officials intend to make a presentation at a crucial meeting of a panel of CDC vaccine advisors next week in which they will link the deaths of 25 children to COVID-19 vaccines.

The claim is said to be based on information in the CDC's Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS), according to a report in The Washington Post, citing four sources. 

A report made to the VAERS system does not necessarily mean that a side effect or death experienced by an individual was caused by a drug or vaccine. The reports are subsequently investigated. 

The CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices is scheduled to meet on Sept. 18 and 19 to discuss recommendations for many shots, including COVID. While the U.S. FDA authorized COVID vaccines for the fall in August, who it is authorized for is restricted compared to prior years. 

ACIP's decision on whether to recommend COVID shots for the groups that the FDA authorized it for is crucial as it determines whether payors are required to cover them.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Winklevoss twins forecast Bitcoin to reach $1 million, disrupting gold
data.description:
Gemini cofounders Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss expressed strong confidence in Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) future, predicting it could reach $1M per coin if it successfully disrupts gold.

“We think Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is gold 2.0. We think there’s easily a 10x from here, and it’s still really early,” the Winklevoss twins said in an interview with CNBC.

The twins highlighted how far the cryptocurrency ecosystem has evolved since they launched Gemini in 2015, when Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was trading at just $380. What began as a platform focused solely on Bitcoin has expanded to offer more than 100 different tokens. Despite this growth, they emphasized, “we think it’s actually just the beginning.”

Crypto exchange Gemini has priced its IPO above the expected range, selling 15.2M shares at $28 each and achieving a valuation exceeding $3B as it lists on Nasdaq (COMP:IND) under the ticker (GEMI).

Gemini’s differentiation in the competitive crypto exchange landscape includes their credit card offering, which provides up to 4% back in cryptocurrency rewards with each purchase, they said.

The Winklevoss twins maintain that Bitcoin (BTC-USD) doesn’t need to function as a transactional currency to succeed.

“We don’t think it actually has to be a transactional currency, just like you’re not trying to buy a cup of coffee with gold. It’s a store of value,” they said, emphasizing Bitcoin’s fixed supply as a key advantage over gold (XAUUSD:CUR), which experiences slight annual expansion through mining.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has recently surpassed $115,000. It is up almost 22% year-to-date, although down 0.32% on Friday.

data.title: U.S. stocks mixed as investors look for direction with Fed decision looming
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were generally mixed on Friday, after the S&P 500 (SP500), the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), and the Dow (DJI) achieved new record closes a day before.

Now investors await the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rate cuts, with the markets pricing odd of a September 25 basis point rate cut at 92.4% next week, according to the CME FedWatch Tool. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in midday trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.4%.

“If the Fed doesn't cut rates multiple times, the economy, especially the job market, will get into even more trouble. If the Fed cuts rates multiple times, the risk of inflation rising again painfully is imminent. In short: the Fed is trapped,” said Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker.

In addition, the U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.4 in September from 58.2 in August, missing the 54.9 consensus.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 6 basis points to 4.09%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.58%.

Among the top gainers on Friday, Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) were +13.2% and +5.4% respectively on the announcement that the latter is preparing a majority cash bid for the entertainment giant.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones (DJI) reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

More on markets:

data.title: Sluggish trends in beer consumption triggers downgrade to Molson Coors, Constellation Brands
data.description:
Reflecting on broader consumer trends, and the Hispanic cohort in particular, Barclays believes the beer category is likely to be weighed down for some time, downgrading  Molson Coors Beverage Company (NYSE:TAP) to Underweight.

While the same fundamentals also apply to Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ), Barclays’ Lauren Lieberman and team think this cautious outlook is adequately captured in Constellation (NYSE:STZ) shares and moved to Equal Weight from Overweight with a corresponding 25% cut to Barclays target price, now at $150.

“Frankly, we struggle to think of a positive catalyst for U.S. beer trends and investor sentiment towards the category…and believe [Molson Coors] has a structurally disadvantage portfolio,” Lieberman and team said in Friday’s note to clients.

For Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ), Lieberman recalibrates her model in respect of the company’s lowered FY26 guidance to assume an expected decrease of 2% to 4% in net sales growth in beer versus prior expectations for sales to be unchanged to up 3%.

Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) also lowered its FY25 guidance to assume macroeconomic headwinds in the category, a sentiment expressed by the company’s CEO on its latest earnings call with analysts.

“These [volatile and challenging] macro impacts in the U.S, have had a disproportionate effect on the lower income and Hispanic consumer. And within beer, these consumer segments have driven the reduction in the number of buyers as well as spend with a shift to singles,” Gavin Hattersley said, adding that combined with weather conditions that dampened demand during the pivotal Memorial Day weekend, these factors have resulted in a much softer U.S. beer industry than previously expected.

Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) and Constellation Brands (STZ) slipped on Friday, weighing on other brewers such as Boston Beer (SAM) and Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD). Constellation shares also hit a fresh low.

data.title: Crypto exchange Gemini's shares estimated to open ~21% above IPO price - report
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange Gemini Space Station (NASDAQ:GEMI) stock is estimated to open at $34 per share, ~21% above its initial public offering price, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

The company led by billionaire twins Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss raised $425 million in an IPO on Thursday, pricing its stock above the previously marketed range.

GEMI offered 15,178,572 shares of class A common stock, priced at $28.00 each, up from the previous marketed range of $24 to $26. Raising the price range underscores the strong demand for crypto-related stocks.

The class A shares are set to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market today.

data.title: Blackstone mulls sale of $1.4B Spanish rental portfolio - report
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) is considering selling a Spanish rental portfolio worth about €1.2B ($1.4B) of homes, according to a media report published Friday.

The private equity giant got Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL) and Eastdil Secured LLC to advise on the potential sale, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the process.

Fidere Patrimonio Socimi SA, part of Blackstone (NYSE:BX), oversees the properties, the people said, adding the companies plan to keep control of the management platform.

Elsewhere in Europe, Blackstone (BX) recently acquired a "trophy" office building in central Paris for about €700M.

Blackstone and JLL did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery bid would likely need to be in low to mid $20s/share - report
data.description:
A Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) would likely need to be in the low to mid $20s/share to be acceptable to the entertainment giant, according to a CNBC report. 

A lot of the money would have to come from billionaire Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, CNBC's David Faber said on the business network on Friday. A bid could come as soon as next week. 

Paramount Skydance  (NASDAQ:PSKY) may be trying to make a bid before Warner Bros.' planned split in an attempt to pre-empt a possible bidding war for the studio and streaming unit that could include mega tech companies like Amazon.com (AMZN) and Apple (AAPL), according to the WSJ report on Thursday.

Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav wants a bidding war to occur between the parties and met with his bankers at Goldman Sachs earlier this week to gauge interest from the tech behemoths as well as Netflix (NFLX), according to a New York Post report on Friday. Zaslav would like the bids to propel Warner Bros. stock to $40 a share. 

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) surged almost 30% on news of the possible takeover bid on Thursday and climbed another 15% on Friday.

data.title: 2 out of 4 companies beat EPS estimates this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Friday, a day after Wall Street notched up record highs, with rate cut bets firmly in place now.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came in higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

“As we approach the end of the week, markets have been in a buoyant mood over the last 24 hours, continuing into this morning's Asian session, with investor attention squarely focused on the slightly higher than expected U.S. August CPI release and the notably higher than expected jobless claims data,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Earnings Roundup: 

Out of the four S&P 500 companies that reported earnings this week, two of them beat EPS estimates, while three of them surpassed revenue expectations. 

Synopsys (SNPS) reported weaker-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results and guidance.

For the period ending July 31, Synopsys said it earned an adjusted $3.39 per share as revenue rose 14% year-over-year to come in at $1.74B. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $3.80 per share on $1.77B in revenue.

Looking ahead, Synopsys expects to earn between $2.76 and $2.80 per share on an adjusted basis, while revenue is forecast to be between $2.23B and $2.26B. 

Kroger (KR) said identical-store sales excluding fuel increased 3.4% for the quarter to come in ahead of the expectation of analysts for a rise of 2.8%. Sales growth during the quarter was led by the pharmacy, e-commerce, and fresh food parts of the business. 

EPS was reported at $1.04 vs. $1.00 consensus and $0.93 a year ago.

Oracle (ORCL) reported a massive surge in remaining performance obligations that overshadowed weaker-than-expected fiscal first-quarter results.

For the period ending Aug. 31, the Safra Catz-led company said it earned an adjusted $1.47 per share on $14.93B in revenue. Analysts had expected the company to earn $1.48 per share on $15.03B in revenue.

Overshadowing the results was a massive 359% surge in total remaining performance obligations during the period to $455B. 

Adobe (ADBE) reported its third-quarter fiscal 2025 financial results that surpassed estimates across the board post-market on Thursday. 

For the quarter ended August 29, the design software company reported adjusted earnings per share of $5.31, which was more than the consensus estimate of $5.18. GAAP EPS was $4.18 compared to the $4.05 estimate.

Revenue for the quarter increased 11% year over year to $5.99B versus the consensus of $5.91B.

Earnings next week:  

Several companies are set to report earnings next week, such as Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (NASDAQ:PLAY) and Radiant (NYSE:RLGT) on Monday, Ferguson (NYSE:FERG), Evolution Petroleum (NYSE:EPM), Flux Power Holdings (NASDAQ:FLUX), and Aspen Group (OTCQB:ASPU) on Tuesday. 

Wednesday will include earnings reports from General Mills (NYSE:GIS), Steelcase (NYSE:SCS), and Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM), with FedEx (NYSE:FDX), Lennar (NYSE:LEN), FactSet Research (NYSE:FDS), and US Gold (NASDAQ:USAU) reporting on Thursday.

data.title: Ares Management plans ~€1.5B credit secondaries transaction - report
data.description:
Ares Management (NYSE:ARES), an alternative asset manager with direct lending activities in Europe, is planning about €1.5 billion worth of credit secondaries transaction, insiders told Bloomberg News.

In the so-called credit secondaries transaction, existing stakes in private credit funds are traded among investors, noted the September 12 report.

Ares' fresh continuation vehicle will hold legacy European loans from its flagship direct-lending strategy, people with knowledge of the matter but not authorized to speak publicly reportedly said.

data.title: Fifth Third Bancorp raises dividend by ~8%
data.description:
• Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ:FITB) declared $0.40/share quarterly dividend, 8.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.37.
  • Forward yield 3.52%
  • Payable Oct. 15; for shareholders of record Sept. 30; ex-div Sept. 30.
  • See FITB Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
